text:
Market today: Risk-on mixed with a real geopolitical shock. U.S. struck Iran after a ceasefire violation — oil is spiking after-hours. That's a macro headwind for tech: risk-off sentiment, potential rate/inflation noise, and chips/growth names sell off hardest when fear spikes. Your margin exposure makes this extra dangerous tonight.

Your holdings: AVGO is your biggest problem — down 16% and on a SELL signal; one bad open and you're near your $336 stop. PLTR is down 13% also flagged SELL — two underwater positions bleeding margin. SPAL is down 16.5% in a 2x leveraged ETF that decays daily — if you haven't sized this tiny, do it now. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history; treat it as a live grenade.

Watch: (1) Oil prices and futures tonight — a sustained spike hits risk appetite and your names open lower. (2) Whether AVGO and PLTR hold above their stops at tomorrow's open.

Bottom line: You have two large losing positions on SELL signals heading into a geopolitical shock — on margin, that combo demands action, not patience.
